title: Priority Road
explanation: You have right-of-way at all intersections ahead until cancelled by another sign.

title: End of Priority Road
explanation: The crossed-out yellow diamond means you no longer have automatic priority. You must now follow normal right-of-way rules.

title: Right Before Left
explanation: At intersections without signs or signals, you must yield to vehicles approaching from your RIGHT. This is the opposite of some US practices.

title: Town Entry Sign
explanation: Yellow sign with town name means you're entering a built-up area. Speed limit drops to 50 km/h (31 mph) unless otherwise posted.

title: Autobahn
explanation: No mandatory speed limit exists, but 130 km/h (80 mph) is recommended.

title: Yield to Oncoming Traffic
explanation: You must yield to oncoming traffic at narrow passages where both directions cannot pass simultaneously.

title: Mandatory Direction: Straight Ahead
explanation: You must continue straight ahead at the next intersection. Turning left or right is not permitted.

title: Mandatory Direction: Turn Right
explanation: You must turn right at the next intersection. Continuing straight or turning left is not permitted.

title: One-Way Street
explanation: All traffic flows in the direction of the arrow.

title: No Vehicles of Any Kind
explanation: No vehicles of any kind may enter, including bicycles. Pedestrians are usually still permitted.

title: No Motor Vehicles
explanation: Multi-track motor vehicles (cars, trucks) are prohibited. Motorcycles, bicycles, and pedestrians may still pass.

title: No Entry
explanation: Entry is forbidden for all vehicles from this direction. Commonly found at the exit end of one-way streets.

title: Speed Limit 60 km/h
explanation: Maximum permitted speed is 60 km/h. This limit applies until cancelled by an end-of-speed-limit sign or a new speed limit.

title: Minimum Speed 30 km/h
explanation: You must maintain at least 30 km/h. Vehicles unable to reach this speed are not permitted on this road.

title: No Overtaking
explanation: Overtaking (passing) other motor vehicles is prohibited until the restriction is lifted.

title: End of Speed Limit 60 km/h
explanation: The 60 km/h speed limit is lifted. The default speed limit for the road type now applies (50 km/h in towns, 100 km/h outside).

title: End of All Restrictions
explanation: All previously posted restrictions (speed limits, no-overtaking zones, etc.) are cancelled at once.

title: No Stopping
explanation: Absolutely no stopping at any time, not even briefly for loading or dropping off passengers.

title: No Parking
explanation: Stopping briefly (up to 3 minutes) for loading/unloading or picking up passengers is allowed, but parking longer is not.

title: Autobahn Exit
explanation: Marks the deceleration lane for an Autobahn exit. Begin reducing speed only after entering the exit lane, not while still on the main carriageway.

title: Lane Shift Panel
explanation: Lanes are shifting ahead, often due to construction. Follow the arrows and observe any embedded restriction signs.

title: Lane Allocation with Speed Limit
explanation: Shows which lanes are available ahead. The marked lane has a specific maximum speed limit.

title: Lane Allocation with Truck Restriction
explanation: Heavy goods vehicles are not permitted in the marked lane and must use the other available lane(s).

title: Lane Allocation with Minimum Speed
explanation: Each lane requires a minimum speed. Slower vehicles must use the lane with the lower minimum speed requirement.

title: Lane Narrowing Panel
explanation: The road narrows ahead. Drivers in the ending lane must merge using the zipper merge principle - vehicles alternate one at a time at the merge point.
